Details: She Who Watches the Web and Weaves in Silence
Contemplation, solitude, sacred pause, seeing through the unseen, weaving one’s own truth in the quiet
Spider-like solitude, reflective stillness, glowing threads of thought, dust and corners, long glances
Appears in moments of retreat, withdrawal, or deep inner processing
White chalk spiral lines forming a spider-like glyph, surrounded by dots like threads in a web
The Weight — not beneath, but *before* her — a symbol to be acknowledged, not carried
Ritual of Seeing — she never passes the weight without bowing her awareness to it
